name: Optimize Ard generated Go decode_pipeline runtime

description: Baseline harness failed because generated Go build did not create expected output path

asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline benchmark harness for generated Go decode_pipeline runtime
asi.failure_mode: autoresearch.sh assumed `ard build --target go --out /tmp/decode-go` creates the binary at that path; the binary was missing when Python tried to run it
asi.rollback_reason: Harness bug, not an optimization result
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect Go backend build output behavior or use the path printed/returned by build; fix autoresearch.sh before collecting baseline

description: Baseline generated Go decode_pipeline runtime after fixing harness

asi.hypothesis: Baseline measurement for generated Go decode_pipeline after harness fixes
asi.baseline_context: Median of 9 direct generated binary executions; output verified as 29678473; checks passed
asi.implementation_state: No optimization changes yet; current target bottleneck likely extern dispatch and reflection-heavy DynamicToList/DynamicToMap in compiler/go decode builtins

description: Remove read lock from extern registry call path

asi.hypothesis: CallExtern is hot in decode_pipeline; avoiding RWMutex RLock/RUnlock on every extern lookup should reduce generated Go decode overhead
asi.result: Small median improvement from 403.95ms to 401.49ms; likely near noise but change is simple
asi.risk: ExternRegistry Call is no longer safe during concurrent map writes; typical generated programs register builtins before Main and only read during execution
asi.next_action_hint: Try larger wins in compiler/go/decode_builtins.go by avoiding reflection in DynamicToList/DynamicToMap and avoiding map copy in ExtractField

description: Fast-path JSON dynamic list/map decode builtins

asi.hypothesis: decode_pipeline JSON values are plain []any and map[string]any, so avoiding reflection and avoiding full map copy in ExtractField should reduce decode overhead
asi.result: Median improved from 401.49ms to 389.63ms (~3.0%); binary size +48 bytes
asi.why_it_worked: ExtractField no longer copies the entire object map for each field lookup; DynamicToList/DynamicToMap use direct type assertions on common JSON shapes before reflective fallback
asi.next_action_hint: Consider avoiding copies entirely for DynamicToList/DynamicToMap or eliminating CallExtern varargs/map lookup for builtins

description: Avoid copying JSON dynamic lists and maps on Go decode fast path

asi.hypothesis: For Go backend Dynamic values, returning underlying []any/map[string]any from decode conversion avoids per-decode allocations/copies in hot JSON decode loops
asi.result: Median improved from 389.63ms to 382.85ms (~1.7%) with checks passing
asi.semantic_risk: Potential aliasing: callers mutating decoded []any/map[string]any can now mutate the underlying Dynamic value in generated Go. Existing tests did not catch this; future review should decide whether performance justifies aliasing or whether copy-on-write semantics are required
asi.next_action_hint: Investigate CallExtern overhead and generated decode code; direct builtin calls or cached extern function pointers may be larger wins without aliasing semantics

description: Fast-path built-in decode externs in CallExtern

asi.hypothesis: Hot decode extern calls spend time in registry map lookup; direct switch cases for built-in decode externs in CallExtern should reduce dispatch overhead
asi.result: Two measurements after change were 382.84ms and 381.58ms versus prior 382.85ms; real effect is very small and likely around noise
asi.tradeoff: Adds hardcoded fast paths for built-in decode extern names in the general extern dispatcher; maintainability cost may be higher than the tiny gain
asi.next_action_hint: Larger wins probably require generated code avoiding CallExtern entirely, or optimizing Result/decoder allocation patterns

description: Check float64 first in Go decode int builtin

asi.hypothesis: JSON numbers arrive as float64 in Go backend Dynamic values, so checking float64 first in builtinDecodeInt avoids failed type-switch cases in the hot path
asi.result: Small median improvement from 381.58ms to 381.10ms; likely modest but directionally consistent
asi.evidence: Confirmed ffi.JsonToDynamic decodes JSON numeric values as float64
asi.next_action_hint: Try reducing allocation from Result/error slices or generated empty list literals in successful decodes

description: Emit append assignment for list_push statements and map index assignment for map_set statements

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding helper calls for push/set statements should reduce generated Go overhead in decode loops
asi.result: Benchmark improved to 379.89ms but checks failed due an updated test expecting map_set statement emission in a case where map_set result is assigned, so code correctly kept helper call there
asi.rollback_reason: checks_failed from test expectation mismatch; experiment needs to be reapplied with only list_push test expectation changed, or a dedicated map_set statement case
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply list_push statement direct append; keep map_set expression tests unchanged unless adding a true ExprStmt map_set fixture

description: Reapply direct list_push/map_set statement emission with partial test update

asi.hypothesis: Direct append/map assignment for ignored push/set results avoids helper call overhead
asi.result: Benchmark improved to 379.58ms but another go_backend test still expected old ListPush helper in a mutable function param fixture
asi.rollback_reason: checks_failed due stale test expectation in TestCompileModuleSourceViaBackendIR_MutableFunctionParamsNative
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply and update both backend_ir_emit_test expectations: list push statements now render as append assignment for locals and dereferenced mutable params

description: Emit direct append/map assignment for ignored push/set results

asi.hypothesis: When list_push/map_set return values are ignored, generated Go can emit native `append` assignment and `map[key] = value` instead of helper calls
asi.result: Median improved from 381.10ms to 380.90ms with checks passing; smaller effect than earlier noisy failed runs but still lower
asi.scope: Only ExprStmt calls are rewritten; expressions that use the returned push/set value still call helpers
asi.next_action_hint: Consider a backend IR or source optimization to preallocate list decoder output capacity, which may reduce append allocations more than helper-call removal

description: Avoid reflective method calls when coercing Ard Result values

asi.hypothesis: CoerceExtern spends substantial time reflect-calling IsOk/UnwrapOk on ardgo.Result values returned by decode externs; reading Result fields directly inside package ardgo should cut hot success-path overhead
asi.result: Large improvement from 380.90ms to 240.35ms (~37%) with checks passing
asi.why_it_worked: DecodeInt/List/Map/Field extern wrappers return ardgo.Result many times; success-path coercion now avoids reflect.MethodByName and Method.Call for Result
asi.risk: Uses unsafe-based field access already present for setting unexported fields; limited to source values whose type is ardgo.Result, with old method-based fallback retained for other result-like types
asi.next_action_hint: Apply analogous direct-field fast path to Maybe coercion or specialize CoerceExtern for common successful decode scalar/list/map results

description: Return DynamicToMap as map[any]any to avoid reflective map coercion

asi.hypothesis: Generated decode.to_map expects [Dynamic:Dynamic] as map[any]any; returning map[string]any forced CoerceExtern to reflect-copy every decoded map
asi.result: Median improved from 240.35ms to 231.49ms (~3.7%); binary size decreased by about 1KB
asi.why_it_worked: builtinDynamicToMap now builds the target map[any]any directly, so CoerceExtern can return the Result without reflective map key/value conversion
asi.tradeoff: Still copies map[string]any JSON objects into map[any]any; direct aliasing is impossible across key types
asi.next_action_hint: Optimize map key sorting/iteration for map[any]any with string keys, or reduce Result coercion allocations further

description: Fast-path string-key maps in Go MapKeys

asi.hypothesis: decode_pipeline iterates maps whose keys are strings; MapKeys can avoid generic mapKeyLess sort comparisons by detecting all-string keys and using sort.Strings
asi.result: Median improved from 231.49ms to 230.07ms (~0.6%) with checks passing
asi.scope: Fast path applies to map[string]V and map[any]V when all dynamic keys are strings; fallback generic ordering remains for mixed/non-string keys
asi.next_action_hint: More substantial wins likely require reducing CoerceExtern reflection for struct errors or avoiding Result coercion in generated extern wrappers entirely

description: Add generic direct type assertion fast path to CoerceExtern

asi.hypothesis: Many extern results already have exactly the requested type, so a generic `value.(T)` check before reflection might avoid CoerceExtern overhead
asi.result: Median regressed from 230.07ms to 230.53ms; direct type assertion overhead did not pay off for this workload
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened; discard and revert
asi.next_action_hint: Do not re-try broad generic type assertion in CoerceExtern. If exact-type fast paths are needed, specialize known hot extern wrappers or avoid CoerceExtern at generation time

description: Use fixed field indexes for Ard Result coercion fast path

asi.hypothesis: The hot CoerceExtern Result fast path still uses FieldByName for source/target Result fields; Result layout is fixed in package ardgo, so Field(0/1/2) should avoid name lookup overhead
asi.result: Large improvement from 230.07ms to 189.36ms (~17.7%) with checks passing
asi.why_it_worked: decode_pipeline performs many successful Result coercions; removing repeated reflective field-name lookup materially reduced per-decode overhead
asi.risk: Assumes Result field order remains value, err, ok; if Result layout changes this fast path must be updated
asi.next_action_hint: Apply analogous fixed field indexes to Maybe coercion, though decode_pipeline may not exercise Maybe; or reduce reflect.TypeFor/isResultType overhead in CoerceExtern

description: Replace strings.HasPrefix in Result/Maybe type checks with manual prefix comparison

asi.hypothesis: isResultType/isMaybeType are hot in CoerceExtern; avoiding strings.HasPrefix might reduce overhead
asi.result: Median regressed from 189.36ms to 190.10ms; manual prefix check did not help
asi.next_action_hint: Do not revisit this micro-optimization; focus on avoiding reflection/CoerceExtern calls entirely or reducing JSON parse overhead

description: Use encoding/json v1 for FFI JsonToDynamic

asi.hypothesis: JSON parsing is hot in decode_pipeline; standard encoding/json might outperform encoding/json/v2 for dynamic object parsing
asi.result: Median regressed badly from 189.36ms to 212.51ms, so json/v2 is faster for this workload
asi.next_action_hint: Keep json/v2. Optimize wrapper/decoder overhead rather than parser implementation

description: Emit direct wrappers for built-in decode externs in Go backend

asi.hypothesis: Generated stdlib extern wrappers still pay CallExtern + CoerceExtern overhead for decode builtins; emitting direct calls to typed ardgo helper functions should eliminate most hot dispatch/coercion overhead without benchmark-specific special casing
asi.result: Very large improvement from 189.36ms to 45.66ms (~75.9%) with checks passing; now near VM runtime for this benchmark
asi.why_it_worked: Successful DecodeInt/List/Map/Field/JsonToDynamic wrappers no longer allocate/coerce generic any results via reflection. Error conversion is still available on error paths via generic helper functions
asi.scope: Special-cases general built-in extern binding names (DecodeString/Int/Float/Bool, DynamicToList/Map, ExtractField, JsonToDynamic), not benchmark paths
asi.risk: Backend now knows selected built-in extern names; adding/changing those builtins requires keeping direct helper functions in sync
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect generated decode_pipeline code and profile remaining time; likely dominated by JSON parser and stdlib decoder closure/list/map allocation

description: Remove obsolete decode fast paths from CallExtern

asi.hypothesis: After direct backend emission for decode extern wrappers, the hardcoded decode switch in CallExtern is unused by this workload and can be removed to simplify runtime and shrink binaries
asi.result: Median slightly improved from 45.66ms to 45.31ms and binary size dropped by ~16.6KB
asi.why_it_worked: Generated decode_pipeline no longer calls CallExtern for these builtins, so removing the switch mainly reduces binary/code footprint without hurting the hot path
asi.risk: Other generated code using generic CallExtern for decode builtins now goes through registry again, but backend should direct-emit these extern wrappers when compiling stdlib modules
asi.next_action_hint: Consider whether the earlier no-lock ExternRegistry.Call change is still worthwhile or if restoring locking improves safety without affecting optimized decode path

description: Restore read lock in ExternRegistry.Call after direct decode extern emission

asi.hypothesis: Since decode_pipeline no longer uses CallExtern for hot decode builtins, restoring the registry read lock might improve safety without hurting the primary metric
asi.result: Median worsened from 45.31ms to 45.54ms and binary grew back by ~16.5KB
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened; keep current no-lock registry from earlier experiments for this branch
asi.next_action_hint: If safety is desired later, consider making the registry immutable after registration or using copy-on-write rather than putting lock overhead back into Call

description: Avoid append-copy emission for empty list and map literals

asi.hypothesis: Generated decode code creates many empty lists/maps for output and errors; copying an empty literal via append([]T(nil), []T{}...) is unnecessary versus emitting []T{} or map[K]V{} directly
asi.result: Median improved from 45.31ms to 45.00ms with checks passing; small but consistent and simpler generated code
asi.why_it_worked: Successful list/map decoders allocate empty error containers and output containers on every decode; avoiding append-copy removes some work on initialization
asi.risk: Only applies to copying empty literals, where aliasing/copy semantics are equivalent for mutable collections because no backing elements exist
asi.next_action_hint: Preallocate decode list/map outputs with len(raw) capacity or avoid allocating errors slices until first error

description: Use UnwrapOk instead of Expect for checked result try success paths

asi.hypothesis: Generated try success paths have already checked IsErr, so using UnwrapOk avoids Expect's redundant branch and panic message argument
asi.result: Median worsened from 45.00ms to 45.33ms, with noisy high samples; optimization did not improve this workload
asi.next_action_hint: Do not retry UnwrapOk substitution without deeper profiling; compiler/inliner may already optimize Expect well enough

description: Emit Dynamic union match cases after concrete cases

asi.hypothesis: Go type switches place `case any` before `case string` for unions like `Str | Dynamic`, causing strings to take the Dynamic arm and perform an unnecessary decodeString call in decode.from_json
asi.result: Median improved from 45.00ms to 44.17ms (~1.9%) with checks passing
asi.why_it_worked: FromJson(payload) now matches the concrete string case before the catch-all Dynamic/any case, avoiding extra result construction and decodeString per iteration
asi.correctness_note: This is also a semantic fix for unions containing Dynamic because the catch-all `any` case should not shadow more specific cases
asi.next_action_hint: Add a focused test for Dynamic case ordering if not already covered; continue looking for generated decode closure allocation overhead

description: Use one-slice string-key MapKeys fast path

asi.hypothesis: MapKeys string fast path currently allocates []string then converts back to []K; sorting one []K slice with string type assertions might reduce allocation overhead
asi.result: Median regressed from 44.17ms to 45.01ms; fewer slices did not compensate for per-comparison type assertions and larger binary
asi.next_action_hint: Keep sort.Strings two-slice implementation. For further MapKeys wins, consider specialized helper functions emitted for concrete map types rather than generic runtime type assertions

description: Use direct Result literals in decode extern helpers

asi.hypothesis: Decode extern helper success paths call generic Ok/Err constructors; direct Result literals inside package ardgo may reduce helper overhead and code size
asi.result: Median slightly improved from 44.17ms to 44.11ms and binary size dropped by ~368 bytes
asi.why_it_worked: Small reduction in generated/runtime helper calls for every successful scalar decode
asi.risk: Uses Result field names inside package ardgo, which is safe unless Result layout changes
asi.next_action_hint: Try direct Result literals in builtinDecodeInt/List/Map/ExtractField themselves or other hot successful Result constructors

description: Use direct Result literals throughout decode builtins

asi.hypothesis: Replacing Ok constructors with direct Result literals in builtin decode success paths may reduce call overhead beyond the extern helper literals
asi.result: Median regressed from 44.11ms to 44.46ms; compiler likely inlines Ok constructors well, and broader literal changes did not help
asi.next_action_hint: Keep direct literals only in decode extern helpers; do not broadly replace Ok in builtin decode functions

description: Rewrite decode error-size matches as if/else in stdlib

asi.hypothesis: Using if/else instead of integer match for `errors.size()` in std_lib/decode.ard may produce simpler generated Go without nested closure expressions on hot successful decode paths
asi.failure_mode: Generated decode_pipeline panicked with nil pointer dereference in Field after changing the match to if/else, indicating different value-return lowering or semantic issue for generic Result-returning if expressions
asi.rollback_reason: Benchmark crashed; semantic/codegen issue, not safe optimization
asi.next_action_hint: Do not rewrite these stdlib matches to if/else without first adding a focused compiler regression test for generic Result-returning if expressions

description: Inline string and int decode logic in typed decode extern helpers

asi.hypothesis: DecodeStringExtern and DecodeIntExtern are hot and currently call builtinDecodeString/Int, creating an intermediate Result before constructing the target Result; inlining success-path logic should remove that overhead
asi.result: Median improved from 44.11ms to 41.82ms (~5.2%) with checks passing
asi.why_it_worked: decode_pipeline decodes 24 ints plus 4 string map keys per iteration; eliminating intermediate Result construction/function calls on those paths adds up
asi.scope: Only typed extern helper success paths are inlined; error paths still build/coerce decode errors
asi.next_action_hint: Inline DynamicToListExtern/DynamicToMapExtern/ExtractFieldExtern/JsonToDynamicExtern success paths or DecodeFloat/Bool if relevant to other benchmarks

description: Inline ExtractFieldExtern success path

asi.hypothesis: ExtractFieldExtern is called several times per decode and can avoid calling builtinExtractField by inlining the map lookup fast path
asi.result: Median regressed from 41.82ms to 42.04ms and binary grew slightly; inlining this helper did not help
asi.next_action_hint: Keep ExtractFieldExtern as a small wrapper; Go compiler likely inlines it adequately or larger code hurts instruction cache

description: Inline DynamicToListExtern common []any path

asi.hypothesis: DynamicToListExtern is called for units and weights each iteration; inlining the common []any path may avoid wrapper overhead
asi.result: Median regressed slightly from 41.82ms to 41.91ms and binary grew; no benefit
asi.next_action_hint: Do not inline DynamicToListExtern common path independently; current builtin wrapper is likely inlined sufficiently

description: Keep only int decode extern inlined; restore string wrapper

asi.hypothesis: The previous string+int inline win may be mostly from int decoding; restoring DecodeStringExtern wrapper could reduce code size without hurting runtime
asi.result: Median regressed from 41.82ms to 42.25ms, so string inline contributes meaningfully despite fewer calls
asi.next_action_hint: Keep both DecodeStringExtern and DecodeIntExtern inlined

description: Check hot decode extern success types before nil

asi.hypothesis: DecodeStringExtern and DecodeIntExtern hot paths receive non-nil string/float64 values, so checking success type before nil may reduce branch overhead
asi.result: Median regressed slightly from 41.82ms to 41.85ms; within noise but not an improvement
asi.next_action_hint: Keep current nil-first DecodeInt/StringExtern layout

description: Fast-path raw string and numeric values before Dynamic conversion

asi.hypothesis: DecodeStringExtern/DecodeIntExtern hot inputs are already raw Go string/float64 JSON values, so checking those before builtinDynamicValue avoids an Encodable type assertion per scalar decode
asi.result: Median improved from 41.82ms to 41.29ms (~1.3%) with checks passing
asi.why_it_worked: The benchmark performs many scalar decodes from JSON dynamic values that do not need Encodable unwrapping
asi.risk: Fallback still calls builtinDynamicValue, preserving behavior for Encodable dynamic wrappers
asi.next_action_hint: Apply similar raw-value fast paths to DynamicToListExtern/DynamicToMapExtern/ExtractFieldExtern only if benchmarking shows benefit; previous naive list/field inlining regressed

description: Fast-path raw []any in DynamicToListExtern

asi.hypothesis: DynamicToListExtern hot inputs are raw []any JSON arrays, so checking before builtinDynamicValue may avoid Encodable assertion overhead
asi.result: Median regressed from 41.29ms to 41.40ms and binary grew slightly
asi.next_action_hint: Do not add raw []any fast path to DynamicToListExtern; unlike scalar decoders it does not pay off

description: Fast-path raw map[string]any in DynamicToMapExtern

asi.hypothesis: DynamicToMapExtern hot input for counts is a raw map[string]any; checking this before builtinDynamicValue may avoid Encodable assertion overhead
asi.result: Median regressed from 41.29ms to 41.50ms; duplication of map-copy logic increased code without improving runtime
asi.next_action_hint: Keep DynamicToMapExtern as wrapper around builtinDynamicToMap

description: Inline JsonToDynamicExtern parser wrapper

asi.hypothesis: JsonToDynamicExtern is called once per decode; inlining builtinJsonToDynamic may remove a wrapper call and direct Result construction overhead
asi.result: Median regressed from 41.29ms to 42.00ms and binary grew; wrapper is likely inlined already or larger code hurts
asi.next_action_hint: Keep JsonToDynamicExtern as wrapper around builtinJsonToDynamic

description: Add single-entry fast path to MapKeys

asi.hypothesis: MapKeys can skip allocation/sort setup for one-entry maps, which may help small dynamic maps generally
asi.result: decode_pipeline maps have more than one entry; median regressed from 41.29ms to 41.58ms due extra branch/code
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid MapKeys branches that do not match this workload; consider specialized emitted map iteration helpers instead

description: Move scalar decode extern error paths to noinline helpers

asi.hypothesis: DecodeStringExtern/DecodeIntExtern hot success paths may inline better if cold error construction/coercion is moved to noinline helpers
asi.result: Median regressed from 41.29ms to 41.38ms and binary grew; noinline split did not help
asi.next_action_hint: Keep scalar decode extern helpers as single functions

description: Split scalar decode extern Encodable fallback into slow helpers

asi.hypothesis: Keeping DecodeStringExtern/DecodeIntExtern hot raw-value paths tiny and moving builtinDynamicValue fallback to helper functions may improve inlining/instruction cache
asi.result: Median regressed from 41.29ms to 41.48ms and binary grew; helper split did not help
asi.next_action_hint: Keep scalar decode raw fast paths in a single function

description: Return sorted string key slice directly for map[string] MapKeys

asi.hypothesis: MapKeys string fast path converts sorted []string back to []K even when K is string; a generic type assertion can return []string directly for map[string]V callers
asi.result: Median improved slightly from 41.29ms to 41.24ms with checks passing
asi.why_it_worked: The benchmark iterates final decoded counts as map[string]int; this avoids one slice allocation/copy on that path while preserving map[any] fallback
asi.risk: Uses a runtime type assertion on []string to []K; safe and falls back for K=any or other key types
asi.next_action_hint: Further MapKeys gains may need backend-emitted concrete key helper calls rather than generic runtime detection

description: Emit MapStringKeys for string-key for-in-map loops

asi.hypothesis: ForInMapStmt can carry key type info so string-key maps call a specialized MapStringKeys helper instead of generic MapKeys runtime detection
asi.result: Median regressed from 41.24ms to 41.68ms despite smaller binary; extra IR/type plumbing or helper split did not improve runtime
asi.next_action_hint: Keep generic MapKeys with []string direct return; do not add ForInMap key-type specialization for this workload

description: Limit raw DecodeIntExtern fast path to float64 JSON numbers

asi.hypothesis: The raw-value fast path in DecodeIntExtern is for JSON numbers, which are float64; raw int/int64 cases can fall through to the existing builtinDynamicValue fallback to keep the hot path smaller
asi.result: Median improved slightly from 41.24ms to 41.20ms with checks passing
asi.why_it_worked: Hot decode_pipeline numeric values are float64, so removing extra raw switch cases reduces fast-path code while preserving int/int64 behavior through fallback
asi.risk: Non-JSON raw int/int64 decode now does one extra builtinDynamicValue call before success, a small tradeoff for JSON-heavy path
asi.next_action_hint: Consider measuring broader benchmarks before finalizing, since this favors JSON dynamic decode workloads

description: Add List::with_capacity and use it for decode list output preallocation

asi.hypothesis: decode.list appends a known number of items, so preallocating output capacity with a List::with_capacity helper should reduce append reallocations
asi.result: Median regressed from 41.20ms to 41.55ms despite checks passing; extra capacity helper plumbing/calls outweighed any append allocation savings for 12-item lists
asi.next_action_hint: Do not add stdlib List::with_capacity for this workload. If preallocation is revisited, prefer backend pattern optimization that emits make([]T,0,len(raw)) without a user-visible helper.

description: Fast-path common Dynamic raw types before Encodable assertion

asi.hypothesis: builtinDynamicValue is hot for JSON Dynamic arrays/maps/fields; most values are already raw Go primitives/collections, so checking common raw types before the Encodable interface assertion should reduce overhead
asi.result: Median improved from 41.20ms to 41.11ms and binary size dropped by ~17KB with checks passing
asi.why_it_worked: DynamicToList/Map/ExtractField still call builtinDynamicValue on raw JSON []any/map[string]any values, avoiding Encodable assertion on those paths helps slightly
asi.risk: Adds type switch to builtinDynamicValue; uncommon raw types now take an extra switch before Encodable check but semantics preserved
asi.next_action_hint: Consider whether all needed raw types are covered; avoid overfitting with benchmark-only shapes

description: Remove scalar decode raw prechecks after Dynamic fast path

asi.hypothesis: After builtinDynamicValue gained raw-type fast paths, DecodeStringExtern/DecodeIntExtern raw prechecks might be redundant and removable
asi.result: Median regressed from 41.11ms to 42.10ms; direct raw prechecks are still important for hot scalar decode
asi.next_action_hint: Keep raw string/float64 prechecks in scalar decode extern helpers even with builtinDynamicValue fast path

description: Emit MapAnyKeys for Dynamic-key for-in-map loops

asi.hypothesis: decode.map iterates a map[Dynamic:Dynamic] (generated as map[any]any); a specialized MapAnyKeys helper should avoid generic MapKeys conversions for dynamic-key maps
asi.result: Median regressed from 41.11ms to 41.25ms despite slightly smaller binary; helper/type-plumbing did not help runtime
asi.next_action_hint: Keep generic MapKeys path; prior MapStringKeys specialization also regressed, suggesting map key iteration is no longer a profitable target

description: Reorder Result fields to put ok flag first

asi.hypothesis: Putting the Result ok flag first may improve field access locality for IsOk/IsErr and the CoerceExtern fixed-index fast path
asi.result: Median regressed from 41.11ms to 41.32ms; field reordering did not help the generated decode workload
asi.next_action_hint: Keep Result field order value, err, ok. Do not retry field layout changes without broader profiling.

description: Export Result fields for direct literal access

asi.hypothesis: Exporting Result fields could allow cheaper direct literals and field access in generated/runtime code while preserving methods
asi.result: After fixing CoerceExtern field-name handling, median regressed from 41.11ms to 41.21ms; no runtime win and API exposure is not justified
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened and change broadens public API surface unnecessarily
asi.next_action_hint: Keep Result fields unexported. If direct generated Result literals are desired, consider a dedicated constructor/intrinsic instead of exporting fields.

description: Replace json/v2 dynamic parsing with manual jsontext parser

asi.hypothesis: A hand-written jsontext token parser for Dynamic values looked slightly faster in a parse-only microcheck and might reduce JsonToDynamic cost
asi.result: Median regressed badly from 41.11ms to 44.82ms; manual parser is slower in full generated backend workload despite parse-only prototype
asi.next_action_hint: Keep encoding/json/v2 JsonToDynamic. Avoid manual JSON parser without deeper profiling and broader correctness coverage.

description: Reorder builtinDynamicValue raw type switch toward collection types

asi.hypothesis: builtinDynamicValue is now hottest for JSON []any/map values, so putting collection cases first may reduce type switch work
asi.result: Median regressed from 41.11ms to 41.26ms; type switch order change did not help
asi.next_action_hint: Keep original raw type switch order in builtinDynamicValue.

description: Invert Result.Expect branch to return on ok first

asi.hypothesis: Result.Expect is called on hot success paths; checking ok first and returning may give the compiler/branch predictor a better shape
asi.result: Median regressed from 41.11ms to 41.26ms; branch shape did not help
asi.next_action_hint: Keep existing Expect implementation.

description: Inline ard/decode::run module calls in Go backend

asi.hypothesis: decode::run is only a shorthand that calls decoder(data); lowering it directly should avoid an extra generated function call for each top-level decode
asi.result: Median regressed from 41.11ms to 41.40ms; Go likely inlines decode.Run already or generated expression shape worsens codegen
asi.next_action_hint: Do not special-case decode::run lowering; focus elsewhere.

description: Emit return block/if expressions as direct statements

asi.hypothesis: Generated decode code returns nested closure expressions for match/if result values; emitting block/if return expressions as direct statements should reduce closure-call overhead
asi.result: Median regressed from 41.11ms to 41.61ms despite smaller binary; direct statement shape hurt runtime or added control-flow overhead
asi.next_action_hint: Do not broadly rewrite return block/if expressions; if revisited, inspect generated code and target a narrower pattern.

description: Use pointer receivers for Result IsOk and IsErr

asi.hypothesis: Generated decode checks call IsOk/IsErr on Result temporaries many times; pointer receivers may avoid copying generic Result structs containing slices/maps and reduce generated code size
asi.result: Median improved from 41.11ms to 40.41ms (~1.7%) and binary size dropped by ~35KB with checks passing
asi.why_it_worked: Result values for decode often contain slices/maps in either value or error type; pointer receiver method calls avoid value receiver copies in hot checks
asi.risk: Go automatically takes addresses for addressable Result variables, but non-addressable Result values may no longer call IsOk/IsErr; current tests pass, so generated call sites appear addressable
asi.next_action_hint: Try pointer receivers for Expect/UnwrapOk/UnwrapErr/Or as they also copy Result values on hot paths

description: Use pointer-based Result ExpectRef for generated try success paths

asi.hypothesis: After pointer IsOk/IsErr improved by avoiding Result copies, generated try success paths can use a new pointer receiver ExpectRef while preserving the public value-receiver Expect API
asi.result: Median improved from 40.41ms to 40.16ms (~0.6%) with checks passing; binary size grew ~1.3KB
asi.why_it_worked: Generated try temporaries are addressable, so ExpectRef avoids copying Result values on hot successful decode unwraps without breaking non-addressable Result.Expect calls
asi.risk: Adds a new public-ish runtime helper method and backend emits it for result try success paths; must keep tests covering generated form
asi.next_action_hint: Consider pointer-based UnwrapErrRef for catch paths only if error-heavy benchmarks matter; decode_pipeline success path unlikely to benefit

description: Use slices.Sort for string-key MapKeys fast path

asi.hypothesis: slices.Sort may be faster or inline better than sort.Strings for the MapKeys all-string fast path
asi.result: Median regressed from 40.16ms to 40.22ms and binary grew slightly
asi.next_action_hint: Keep sort.Strings for string key sorting.

description: Special-case map[string]V in generic MapKeys

asi.hypothesis: MapKeys can avoid per-key type assertions for concrete map[string]V by detecting the map type up front
asi.result: Median regressed from 40.16ms to 40.49ms and binary grew significantly; generic map type assertion is costly or hurts codegen
asi.next_action_hint: Keep existing all-string detection and []string direct return; avoid map[string]V generic type assertion specialization.

description: Use ExpectRef for addressable result_expect method calls

asi.hypothesis: Result match success bindings use result_expect and are very hot in decode list/map loops; using ExpectRef for addressable subjects should avoid Result copies like try success paths did
asi.result: Median improved from 40.16ms to 39.36ms (~2.0%) with checks passing
asi.why_it_worked: decode.List and decode.Map perform result matches for every element/key/value; their generated `Expect("expected ok")` calls now avoid copying Result values
asi.scope: Only addressable result_expect subjects use ExpectRef; non-addressable expressions still use value-receiver Expect
asi.next_action_hint: Consider similar pointer-ref emission for result_or if any hot code uses it; or evaluate pointer receivers for Maybe methods in decode nullable/path workloads

description: Use pointer UnwrapOkRef instead of ExpectRef after result checks

asi.hypothesis: Generated code calls ExpectRef only after checking result success, so a pointer UnwrapOkRef without branch/message may be faster
asi.result: Median regressed from 39.36ms to 39.48ms and binary size grew substantially; removing the branch did not help codegen
asi.next_action_hint: Keep ExpectRef for result_expect/try success despite redundant branch; compiler codegen is better in this workload.

description: Use explicit false comparison in Result.IsErr

asi.hypothesis: Changing IsErr from `!r.ok` to `r.ok == false` might alter codegen/branch prediction for hot error checks
asi.result: Median regressed from 39.36ms to 39.57ms; no benefit
asi.next_action_hint: Keep IsErr as logical negation.

description: Use no-argument ExpectOkRef for generated result success unwraps

asi.hypothesis: ExpectRef's panic message parameter appears to escape in gc diagnostics; a no-argument ExpectOkRef with a constant panic string may avoid that overhead while retaining the successful branch shape
asi.result: Median regressed from 39.36ms to 39.50ms and binary size grew ~51KB; avoiding the message parameter did not help codegen
asi.next_action_hint: Keep ExpectRef with explicit messages; do not replace success unwraps with no-arg ExpectOkRef.

description: Emit implicit-address ExpectRef method calls

asi.hypothesis: For addressable Result temporaries, emitting `res.ExpectRef(...)` instead of `(&res).ExpectRef(...)` lets Go handle the implicit address and may produce cleaner SSA/codegen
asi.result: Median improved from 39.36ms to 39.12ms (~0.6%) with checks passing and unchanged binary size
asi.why_it_worked: Generated code uses many Result success unwraps; the simpler selector form appears to codegen slightly better than explicit unary address expressions
asi.scope: Only addressable generated Result success paths use implicit pointer method calls; non-addressable fallbacks still use value Expect
asi.next_action_hint: Try the same implicit-address style for other pointer receiver Result methods if explicit address appears elsewhere.

description: Omit automatic discard assignment for every backend bind

asi.hypothesis: The Go backend emits `_ = local` after every bind to suppress unused-variable errors; removing these redundant dead assignments may reduce generated code overhead in hot decode blocks
asi.result: Median regressed from 39.12ms to 39.22ms; Go likely eliminates these stores and the changed source shape did not improve runtime
asi.next_action_hint: Keep bind discard assignments unless implementing a more targeted dead-code cleanup validated by profiling.

description: Return []any directly for all-string MapKeys with Dynamic keys

asi.hypothesis: For map[any]any values whose keys are all strings, MapKeys can build and return a []any directly instead of doing per-element generic type assertions from string to K
asi.result: Median regressed from 39.12ms to 39.21ms; the extra branch/code outweighed any conversion benefit
asi.next_action_hint: Keep current MapKeys []string direct return only for K=string; avoid Dynamic-key specializations.

description: Introduce temporaries for non-addressable result_expect binds

asi.hypothesis: Top-level decode results call value-receiver Expect on non-addressable function returns; binding the Result to a temp and using pointer ExpectRef may avoid copying Result values
asi.result: Median regressed from 39.12ms to 39.47ms; extra temporaries/source shape outweighed copy avoidance for function-return result_expect binds
asi.next_action_hint: Keep non-addressable result_expect as value Expect; pointer ExpectRef only helps existing addressable temporaries.

description: Emit direct Print extern wrapper in Go backend

asi.hypothesis: Generated programs call io::print once per run via CallExtern; emitting a direct PrintExtern helper can avoid registry dispatch and slightly reduce startup/end overhead
asi.result: Median regressed from 39.12ms to 39.28ms despite a tiny binary-size drop; one print call is not a profitable target
asi.next_action_hint: Do not add direct Print wrapper for decode_pipeline performance; if extern registration/startup is targeted, address RegisterBuiltinExterns more broadly.

description: Preallocate default extern registry map

asi.hypothesis: Every generated Go process registers many builtin externs at startup; preallocating the extern registry map may reduce startup overhead included in the benchmark
asi.result: Median regressed from 39.12ms to 39.29ms; map growth during registration is not a profitable bottleneck or noise dominates
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid registry preallocation as a decode_pipeline optimization.

description: Use direct Result literals in JsonToDynamic builtin

asi.hypothesis: JsonToDynamic is called once per decode iteration; replacing Ok/Err constructor calls with direct Result literals inside ardgo may shave wrapper overhead
asi.result: Median regressed from 39.12ms to 39.55ms; constructors are inlined well and direct literals hurt codegen/noise
asi.next_action_hint: Keep builtinJsonToDynamic using Ok/Err constructors.

description: Use insertion sort for small all-string MapKeys

asi.hypothesis: decode_pipeline sorts several four-key maps; a small-slice insertion sort in the all-string MapKeys fast path may beat sort.Strings call overhead generally for small maps
asi.result: Median regressed from 39.12ms to 39.53ms; hand-written small sort worsened codegen/runtime
asi.next_action_hint: Keep sort.Strings even for small all-string maps.

description: Decode maps through string-key Dynamic maps

asi.hypothesis: JSON object Dynamic values already use string keys; decode.map can avoid copying map[string]any into map[any]any and avoid Dynamic-key MapKeys conversions by using a string-key Dynamic map helper
asi.result: Benchmark improved from 39.12ms to 38.51ms, but checks failed in bytecode/vm because the new DynamicToStringMap extern was not implemented/registered for VM
asi.rollback_reason: Backpressure checks failed; cannot keep partial Go-only stdlib extern change
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply with VM/JS/runtime support for DynamicToStringMap, run go generate ./bytecode/vm, and verify formatter/checker/vm tests.

description: Decode maps through string-key Dynamic maps

asi.hypothesis: JSON object Dynamic values already have string keys; decode.map can use a string-key Dynamic map helper to avoid copying map[string]any into map[any]any and reduce Dynamic-key MapKeys conversions
asi.result: Median improved from 39.12ms to 38.94ms with full checks passing; earlier 38.51ms checks-failed run showed same direction but more noise
asi.why_it_worked: Go DynamicToStringMapExtern aliases map[string]any JSON objects directly, and generated decode.map now iterates map[string]any keys before wrapping each key as Dynamic for the user-supplied key decoder
asi.scope: General stdlib decode.map optimization for JSON/object maps with string keys; VM and JS support added for the new DynamicToStringMap extern
asi.risk: decode.map now treats JSON/object keys as strings explicitly, which matches JSON object semantics but changes the internal Dynamic map shape used by the decoder implementation
asi.next_action_hint: Investigate direct Go backend emission for Dyn::from_str inside decode.map to avoid CallExtern/registry overhead on each map key.

description: Emit direct primitive-to-Dynamic extern bodies

asi.hypothesis: After decode.map switched to string-key maps, each key is wrapped with Dyn::from_str; direct-emitting primitive Dynamic conversion externs avoids CallExtern registry dispatch on every map key and helps other generated Go programs too
asi.result: Median improved from 38.94ms to 36.59ms (~6.0%) with checks passing and slightly smaller binary
asi.why_it_worked: dynamic.FromStr now compiles to `return val` instead of CallExtern+CoerceExtern; decode_pipeline calls this for every decoded map key
asi.scope: General Go backend direct extern emission for Str/Int/Float/Bool/Void-to-Dynamic conversions
asi.risk: Backend now knows more builtin extern bindings; keep direct behavior in sync with ard/dynamic stdlib and runtime semantics
asi.next_action_hint: Consider direct-emitting ListToDynamic/MapToDynamic only if benchmarks use encode/dynamic construction; decode_pipeline likely does not.

description: Fast-path raw map[string]any in DynamicToStringMapExtern

asi.hypothesis: DynamicToStringMapExtern hot inputs are already raw JSON map[string]any values, so checking that before builtinDynamicValue avoids an extra raw-type switch/Encodable assertion
asi.result: Median improved from 36.59ms to 36.49ms with checks passing; binary grew by 64 bytes
asi.why_it_worked: decode.map now calls DynamicToStringMapExtern for counts; the common raw JSON object path can return immediately
asi.scope: General fast path for string-key Dynamic map decoding in generated Go
asi.next_action_hint: Try similar raw map[string]any fast path in ExtractFieldExtern, which also receives JSON objects for field lookups, although prior ExtractField inlining regressed.

description: Fast-path raw map[string]any in ExtractFieldExtern

asi.hypothesis: ExtractFieldExtern hot inputs are raw JSON map[string]any objects for units/weights/counts field access, so checking that before builtinDynamicValue may avoid raw-type switch overhead
asi.result: Median regressed slightly from 36.49ms to 36.53ms and binary grew; prior ExtractField inlining/regression pattern still holds
asi.next_action_hint: Keep ExtractFieldExtern as a wrapper around builtinExtractField; do not duplicate its raw map path.

description: Use direct Result literal in DynamicToStringMapExtern fast path

asi.hypothesis: The new hot DynamicToStringMapExtern raw map fast path can avoid an Ok constructor by returning a direct Result literal inside ardgo
asi.result: Median regressed from 36.49ms to 36.60ms; Ok constructor codegen remains better for this helper
asi.next_action_hint: Keep Ok constructor in DynamicToStringMapExtern fast path.

description: Limit direct Dynamic conversion extern emission to StrToDynamic

asi.hypothesis: decode_pipeline only uses Dyn::from_str after the string-key map change, so restricting direct Dynamic conversion emission to StrToDynamic might reduce unused generated code and improve runtime/code size
asi.result: Median regressed from 36.49ms to 36.64ms; keeping all primitive/Void direct Dynamic conversions is better or noise favors the broader shape
asi.next_action_hint: Keep direct emission for all primitive/Void-to-Dynamic externs.

description: Lower primitive Dynamic constructors as Go intrinsics

asi.hypothesis: Inlining Dyn::from_str at lowering time should remove the generated dynamic.FromStr function call and reduce boxing/allocation in decode.map key decoding
asi.result: Median regressed from 36.49ms to 36.55ms despite smaller binary; direct module-call lowering did not improve runtime
asi.next_action_hint: Keep direct extern bodies in ard/dynamic but do not lower dynamic constructors as expression intrinsics.

description: Preallocate empty list binds before append loops

asi.hypothesis: decode.list appends a known raw list length into an initially empty output list; a backend peephole can emit make([]T,0,len(raw)) for empty list binds that are appended inside following for-in-list loops
asi.result: Median regressed slightly from 36.49ms to 36.52ms; direct preallocation did not overcome extra make/capacity overhead for 12-element lists
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue list preallocation for this workload unless deeper allocation profiling shows a narrower profitable pattern.

description: Remove map[any]any from builtinDynamicValue raw fast path

asi.hypothesis: After decode.map switched to string-key maps, map[any]any is no longer hot in builtinDynamicValue; removing it from the raw type switch may shrink the common switch path
asi.result: Median regressed from 36.49ms to 36.54ms; keeping map[any]any in the raw fast path remains better or neutral
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the existing builtinDynamicValue raw type set.

description: Add scalar decode externs that return error lists directly

asi.hypothesis: decode.string and decode.int immediately wrap scalar decode errors into single-element error lists; dedicated builtins returning Result[T, []Error] can avoid the intermediate Result[T, Error], try/ExpectRef success unwrap, and Ok rewrap on hot scalar decode paths
asi.result: Median improved from 36.49ms to 35.58ms (~2.5%) with checks passing; binary grew ~1.7KB
asi.why_it_worked: decode_pipeline decodes many ints and map string keys; generated decode.String/Int now delegate to direct DecodeStringErrors/DecodeIntErrors externs instead of try-wrapping DecodeString/DecodeInt
asi.scope: General stdlib scalar decoder optimization for Go, JS, and VM support; existing Error-returning private decoders remain for from_json raw Dynamic handling
asi.risk: Adds additional FFI surface and backend direct cases that must stay consistent with decode Error shape
asi.next_action_hint: Try analogous DecodeFloatErrors/DecodeBoolErrors for completeness only if broader benchmarks use them; decode_pipeline likely unaffected.
